CSCI-2963.description: This course introduces the fundamental principles and practices of ethical hacking and web security. Students will examine common web application vulnerabilities, including injection attacks, cross-site scripting (XSS), authentication flaws, business logic vulnerabilities, and access control weaknesses. The course emphasizes both offensive techniques—such as reconnaissance, vulnerability assessment, and controlled exploitation—and defensive strategies for securing web systems. Through hands-on exercises and guided labs, students will learn how to identify security weaknesses, understand how they are exploited, and implement practical mitigation techniques. Ethical, legal, and professional responsibilities in cybersecurity are emphasized throughout the course.
CSCI-2963.name: Ethical Hacking & Web Security [6-Week Jul 6-Aug 14]

CSCI-4962.name: Intro Deep Learn Nat Lang Gen [6-Week May 18-Jun 26]
